title: Feature Importance Audit Snapshot Correlation (Art. 13)

compliance.assurance_rationale: Uplifted from contributing to partial: UC provides meaningful monitoring evidence for Art.13.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that EU AI Act Art.13 (Transparency and information) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.21.9: Feature Importance Audit Snapshot Correlation (Art. 13).
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.21.9' running on index=ml_explain sourcetype=ai:shap:export — job_id, model_version, artifact_uri, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

app: Splunk Machine Learning Toolkit (2890), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621)
spl:
index=ml_explain sourcetype="ai:shap:export" earliest=-30d
| stats latest(_time) as last_export by model_version
| join type=left model_version [
    search index=ai_serving sourcetype="ai:gateway:access" earliest=-30d
    | stats dc(model_version) as versions_seen by model_version
  ]
| eval stale=if(isnull(last_export) OR (now()-last_export)>2592000,1,0)
| where stale=1
| table model_version, last_export, versions_seen
description: Correlates SHAP export job completion with model_version to retain feature importance audit snapshots for transparency reviews.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for EU AI Act compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes ai_serving, ml_explain and sourcetypes ai:gateway:access, ai:shap:export, ai:shap:export`. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Schedule SHAP export on each release; (2) store artifact_uri in object storage with WORM; (3) alert if model_version in prod without export in 7d; (4) document in technical file. Indexes required: `index=ai_serving`, `index=ml_explain`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=ai:gateway:access`, `sourcetype=ai:shap:export`, `sourcetype=ai:shap:export``.
visualization: Timeline (exports), Table (stale models), Single value (stale count)
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Machine Learning Toolkit (2890), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=ml_explain` `sourcetype=ai:shap:export` — job_id, model_version, artifact_uri.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Schedule SHAP export on each release; (2) store artifact_uri in object storage with WORM; (3) alert if model_version in prod without export in 7d; (4) document in technical file.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=ml_explain sourcetype="ai:shap:export" earliest=-30d
| stats latest(_time) as last_export by model_version
| join type=left model_version [
    search index=ai_serving sourcetype="ai:gateway:access" earliest=-30d
    | stats dc(model_version) as versions_seen by model_version
  ]
| eval stale=if(isnull(last_export) OR (now()-last_export)>2592000,1,0)
| where stale=1
| table model_version, last_export, versions_seen
```

Understanding this SPL

**Feature Importance Audit Snapshot Correlation (Art. 13) (EU AI Act)** — Correlates SHAP export job completion with model_version to retain feature importance audit snapshots for transparency reviews.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=ml_explain` `sourcetype=ai:shap:export` — job_id, model_version, artifact_uri. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Machine Learning Toolkit (2890), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: ml_explain; **sourcetype**: ai:shap:export. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=ml_explain, sourcetype="ai:shap:export", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` aggregates events by **model_version**, producing one row per unique combination for trend analysis and threshold evaluation.
• Correlates data from a subsearch to enrich the primary result set with additional context.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **stale** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Filters the current rows with `where stale=1` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Output fields: last_export, model_version, stale, versions_seen.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Timeline (exports), Table (stale models), Single value (stale count)
grandmaExplanation: We watch for correlates shap export job completion with model_version to retain feature importance audit snapshots for transparency reviews. This helps maintain steady compliance posture and reduces manual review effort.
